ogTitle: Sheep Hills vs Shelbourne: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sheep Hills and Shelbourne.
heading: Sheep Hills vs Shelbourne — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sheep Hills, Victoria and Shelbourne, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Shelbourne has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Sheep Hills (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sheep Hills or Shelbourne safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sheep Hills has a flood risk score of 60/100, while Shelbourne scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sheep Hills scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Shelbourne scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sheep Hills or Shelbourne?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Shelbourne has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
